_about: Decode-step latency + peak KV memory: fp16 attention vs TurboQuant fused M2-warp kernel vs naive dequant-then-attend. Produced by nrp/latency_bench.py on NRP (job tq-kv-latency, 2026-06-29). head_dim=128, key_bits=4.
takeaway: The fused M2-warp kernel is ~12-17x faster than naive dequant and within ~1.5x of fp16 at long context (H32 S16384: fp16 0.383 / warp 0.575 / dequant 9.206 ms). Compressed-KV decode is viable: the kernel removes the dequant bottleneck (dequant scales O(S), warp stays ~flat) while the cache stores 4-bit keys (4x less than the fp16_mem column). Kernel-launch overhead dominates at short context, where fp16 still wins; the warp kernel pays off as S grows.
